expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: No coverage matrix file was produced. The test file contains only Java test code with no accompanying coverage matrix document.

expectations.text: Every sequential behavior step's on_failure path has a dedicated test (invalid_sync, unsupported_version, length_mismatch, checksum_failed, unknown_type)
expectations.evidence: invalid_sync: shouldFailOnWrongFirstSyncByte, shouldFailOnWrongSecondSyncByte. unsupported_version: shouldFailOnVersionZero, shouldFailOnVersionTwo, shouldFailOnVersionMaxByte. length_mismatch: shouldFailWhenPayloadLengthExceedsInputSize, shouldFailWhenInputTooShort. checksum_failed: shouldFailOnIncorrectChecksum, shouldFailChecksumWithValidStructure. unknown_type: shouldFailOnUnknownMessageType, shouldFailOnMessageTypeZero.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (null/empty raw_data, too-short raw_data) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: Null: shouldHandleNullInput (line 493). Empty: shouldHandleEmptyInput (line 504). Too-short: shouldFailForInputSmallerThanMinimum (line 539) with 11 bytes, shouldHandleSingleByteInput (line 519) with 1 byte.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for raw_data length (4 bytes minimum, 1024 bytes maximum, 3 bytes too short, 1025 bytes too long)
expectations.evidence: Tests cover 12 bytes minimum (shouldPassMinimumSizeRequirement, line 556), 11 bytes too short (shouldFailForInputSmallerThanMinimum, line 539), 1024 bytes maximum (shouldAcceptMaximumSizeInput, line 578), and 1025 bytes too long (shouldRejectInputLargerThanMaximum, line 601). However, there is no test at 4 bytes or 3 bytes specifically. The assertion asks for 4 bytes minimum boundary, but the implementation uses 12 bytes as minimum.

expectations.text: Union return type handled: tests assert on specific Success or Error variant fields, not just non-null
expectations.evidence: The ParseResult interface uses a single flat interface (not a sealed union) with methods like getMessageType(), getErrorCode(), getByteOffset(). Tests check isSuccess()/assertFalse(result.isSuccess()) and then access fields, but several success-path tests wrap assertions in 'if (result.isSuccess())' guards (lines 572, 594, 637, 659, 689, 811, 850, 889), meaning if the result is an error, the test silently passes without asserting anything meaningful. This is a weak assertion pattern.

expectations.text: No test uses only 'assertDoesNotThrow' or 'assertNotNull' as its sole assertion
expectations.evidence: shouldReturnAllSuccessFields (line 796): assertions are guarded by 'if (result.isSuccess())' and use assertTrue(result.getMessageType() >= 0), assertNotNull(result.getPayload()), assertTrue(result.getSequenceNumber() >= 0) - these are essentially structural/not-null checks, not specific value assertions. shouldReturnPayloadAsMap (line 836): 'if (result.isSuccess()) { assertNotNull(result.getPayload()); assertTrue(result.getPayload() instanceof java.util.Map); }' - solely structural assertions guarded by an if-success check.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: Test names use @DisplayName annotations describing scenarios: 'Should parse complete valid message and return Success', 'Should return invalid_sync error when first byte is wrong', 'Should return length_mismatch when payload_length is too large'. Method names also descriptive: shouldParseValidMessage, shouldFailOnWrongFirstSyncByte.

expectations.text: Happy path test: valid message parsed correctly with verified payload fields
expectations.evidence: shouldParseValidMessage (line 43) asserts assertTrue(result.isSuccess()), assertEquals(1, result.getMessageType()), assertEquals(1, result.getSequenceNumber()), assertNotNull(result.getPayload()). However, payload fields are only checked with assertNotNull - no specific payload field values are verified. shouldParseMessageWithPayload (line 66) similarly only uses assertNotNull(result.getPayload()) without verifying actual payload content/field values.
